description: Python frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION('slice', start, stop, step) for subscript slices. Currently goes symbolic. Need VM builtin handling start/stop/step for strings, lists, and heap arrays.
title: VM builtin: string/array slicing for Python (a[1:3], a[::2])

description: Lua has no native class system. The Rosetta method chaining test uses a table-based OOP pattern (Counter.new(), Counter.increment(self)) with explicit self-passing. This pattern produces symbolic results because the VM doesn't recognise the table-as-object convention. The Lua frontend would need to detect self-returning table methods and track the table identity through the call chain. This may be a lower priority since Lua's OOP is purely conventional, not language-level.
title: Lua table-based OOP method chaining produces symbolic results

description: C++ frontend lowers 'return *this' as LOAD_FIELD [this_reg, '*'], treating the unary dereference operator as a field access on a field literally named '*'. Should resolve to just 'this' since our VM references aren't real pointers. Blocks method chaining (return *this pattern). See _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py.
title: C++ *this dereference lowered as LOAD_FIELD instead of no-op

description: Scala functions with block bodies (def f(): Int = { expr }) do not return the last expression value. The lower_function_def handler calls ctx.lower_block(body_node) for block-type bodies, which iterates children but discards the last expression result. A standalone 'this' or 'this.count' at the end of a block should be the implicit return value. This affects method chaining (return this) and value-returning methods. Workaround: use explicit 'return' keyword. See test_rosetta_method_chaining.py where scala remains in _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES.
title: Scala implicit return from blocks not working

description: Tree-sitter JS grammar doesn't provide an 'update' named field for C-style for loops. lower_c_style_for in interpreter/frontends/common/control_flow.py gets None for update_node, causing infinite loops. JS/TS for-loops with updates need positional extraction or custom handling. Discovered during array accumulate Rosetta test.
title: JS/TS for-loop update field not extracted by tree-sitter

description: Lua models objects as tables created in factory functions (local self = {count = 0}), lowered as NEW_OBJECT + STORE_INDEX. Method calls are positional (Counter.increment(counter)) not method syntax. The VM can't track heap objects through dynamic table initialization and function-based dispatch. This is an architectural limitation — no class/constructor mechanism for Lua to hook into. Unlike the C++/PHP bugs, this requires deeper VM support for table-based OOP patterns. See _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py.
title: Lua method chaining: dynamic table initialization not tracked through VM

description: Fixed: Frontend emits STORE_INDEX for arr(i)=5 writes. VM resolves CALL_FUNCTION on heap arrays for reads (apply semantics). Commit b39ab69.
title: Scala arr(i) emits CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX

description: Method chaining (return this/self) produces symbolic results for C++, C#, Java, Kotlin, Lua, PHP, Scala. The VM doesn't resolve 'return this' back to the object reference correctly in these frontends. Discovered during method chaining Rosetta test.
title: Method chaining: return-this produces symbolic for 7 languages

description: ~25 call sites across 10 OOP frontends still pass raw class_name strings (from ctx.node_text()) to seed_register_type, seed_param_type, seed_var_type. These should be wrapped in ScalarType(class_name) to complete the string→TypeExpr migration. Covers this/self seeding and cast expression handlers in JS, Java, C#, C++, Kotlin, Scala, PHP, and expression files.
title: Wrap remaining raw class_name strings in ScalarType() at seed call sites

description: Ruby supports range-based slicing (a[1..3]) and index+length slicing (a[1,2]). Check if frontend emits slice calls and add VM support.
title: VM builtin: string/list slicing for Ruby (a[1..3], a[1,2])

description: ~100 existing call sites across 15 frontends pass raw strings to seed_register_type, seed_var_type, and seed_param_type. Migrate them to pass TypeExpr objects directly (ScalarType, metatype, etc.) instead of going through parse_type() roundtrips. The seed methods already accept str | TypeExpr, so this is incremental.
title: Migrate existing seed call sites from string to TypeExpr

description: Both P1s (C1: test_typed_params, C2: test_adjacent_errors_merged) were already resolved with proper assertions. Verified in code.
title: Test audit: 2 remaining P1s from audit #14

description: From Programming Patterns Audit #1 (2026-03-07). ~15 for-loop mutations remain in vm.py, executor.py, cfg.py, type_graph.py — left as-is because they are inherently imperative shell. Also ~120 defensive None checks (systemic/low) and magic strings (incremental). See memory/code_review_audit1.md.
title: Code review: remaining for-loop mutations (~15 in vm.py/executor.py/cfg.py)

description: Kotlin uses method calls for slicing (substring, subList, slice). Check if these are lowered and add VM builtin support.
title: VM builtin: string/list slicing for Kotlin (substring, subList)

description: ADR-100 written and committed (654531f). Three phases: frontend alignment → inference dispatcher → TypeGraph wiring. Ready for implementation.
title: Interface-aware type inference: cross-language enhancement

description: Done: commit 12bea65. Interface methods now produce FUNC_DEF labels with seeded return types.
title: Phase 1a: Java interface lowering — process method bodies
Phase 1b: C# interface lowering — process method bodies

description: Change TS lower_interface_decl to lower interface method/property/call/construct/index signatures as function definitions. Covers 5 P1 gaps from frontend-lowering-gaps.md (method_signature, property_signature, call_signature, construct_signature, index_signature). File: typescript.py. Tests: unit tests per signature type.
title: Phase 1c: TypeScript interface lowering — process method signatures

description: Add dedicated Kotlin interface handler that processes method declarations (currently no handler exists). Mirror class lowering pattern. File: kotlin/declarations.py + kotlin frontend dispatch. Tests: unit test verifying interface methods produce FUNC_DEF labels.
title: Phase 1d: Kotlin interface lowering — add handler

description: Add Go interface type handler that extracts method signatures from interface definitions. Currently only struct types handled. File: go/declarations.py + go frontend dispatch. Tests: unit test verifying interface method set lowering.
title: Phase 1e: Go interface lowering — extract method set

description: Extend _infer_call_method() in type_inference.py to walk interface_implementations when direct class_method_types lookup fails. Thread interface_implementations from TypeEnvironment into _InferenceContext. ~15 LOC. Tests: unit test — class implements interface, method call on class-typed var resolves return type via interface. Integration test: TS/Java/C# programs with interface-typed variables.
title: Phase 2: Add interface chain walk to _infer_call_method()

description:
extend_with_interfaces() adds class→interface edges to the TypeGraph so is_subtype_expr() works across interface hierarchies. Currently no production code consumes this. This epic tracks the three scenarios that would require it, each as a child story.

Prerequisite: extend_with_interfaces() itself works (tested in test_type_graph.py). The work here is wiring it into the inference/execution pipeline AND building the consumers.

Intersects with red-dragon-jn0 (pattern matching) — type narrowing from instanceof/is checks is the most likely first consumer.
title: TypeGraph interface subtyping: production consumers

description:
When the inference engine encounters instanceof (Java/TS), is (C#/Kotlin), or pattern match with type checks (Scala/Rust), it should narrow the variable's type within the true branch.

Example: if (x instanceof Animal) { x.speak(); } — inside the branch, x should be narrowed from Object to Animal. is_subtype_expr() is needed to validate that the narrowing is sound (e.g., Dog narrows to Animal because Dog <: Animal).

Requires:
1. Wire extend_with_interfaces() into infer_types() to build the augmented TypeGraph
2. Add type narrowing logic to _infer_branch_if or a new narrowing pass
3. Track narrowed types per-scope (similar to TypeScript's control flow narrowing)

This is the most likely first consumer of is_subtype_expr(). Intersects heavily with red-dragon-jn0 (pattern matching).
title: Type narrowing in conditionals (instanceof/is/match)

description:
TypeResolver.resolve_assignment() currently only handles primitive coercions (Int→Float, Bool→Int). For reference types, Animal a = new Dog() should be a no-op (no coercion needed) when Dog <: Animal via is_subtype_expr().

Currently this case is handled implicitly (VM doesn't coerce objects), but explicit subtype checking would enable:
- Compile-time warnings for incompatible assignments
- Covariant container checks (List<Dog> assignable to List<Animal> with proper variance)

Lower priority than type narrowing — the VM works without this today.
title: Assignment compatibility for reference types
Method overload resolution via subtype matching

description: Change _scan_classes to accumulate all function references per method name instead of overwriting. class_methods value type changes from str to list[str]. Update all consumers of class_methods to handle the list.
title: Registry: accumulate overloads in _scan_classes

description: func_signatures is keyed by function name, so overloads overwrite each other. Add func_signatures_by_label (label → FunctionSignature) to TypeEnvironment for overload-safe signature lookup. Keep existing name-keyed func_signatures for backward compat. The overload resolver in .7.3.2 will use the label-keyed version.
title: Type inference: label-keyed func_signatures for overload support

description: Change func_signatures value type from FunctionSignature to list[FunctionSignature]. Add get_func_signature(name, index=0, class_name=ScalarType) accessor to TypeEnvironment. Migrate all direct dict access (70+ sites in tests, inference, executor) to use the accessor. Default index=0 preserves existing behavior.
title: func_signatures: migrate to list[FunctionSignature] with accessor wrapper

description: Add method_signatures: dict[ScalarType, dict[str, list[FunctionSignature]]] to TypeEnvironment, scoped by class TypeExpr. Populate from class_method_types during inference (already uses ScalarType keys). Update get_func_signature wrapper to route class-scoped lookups to method_signatures. Executor wraps type_hint string in ScalarType at the lookup boundary.
title: func_signatures: class-scoped method_signatures with ScalarType keys

description: Add convenience methods to TypeEnvironmentBuilder for appending overloaded signatures cleanly during type inference. E.g. add_func_signature(name, sig), add_method_signature(class_type, name, sig). Replaces raw dict manipulation in _infer_const.
title: func_signatures: convenience mutators on TypeEnvironmentBuilder

description: Stop copying class method types into the flat name-keyed func_return_types/func_param_types dicts. Class methods now only populate method_signatures. Flat func_signatures is reserved for standalone functions.
title: Remove class method pollution from flat func_signatures

description: FunctionSignature has no structural way to distinguish instance methods from static methods from top-level functions. The only signal is a fragile string convention (param named 'this'). Add a FunctionKind enum and a 'kind' field to FunctionSignature, plus a callable_params property that excludes 'this' for instance methods. Frontends set the kind during lowering/inference based on class context.
title: FunctionSignature: add FunctionKind enum (UNBOUND/INSTANCE/STATIC)

description: In _handle_call_method, when multiple overloads exist: 1) Filter by arity 2) If multiple arity matches, use TypeEnvironment + is_subtype_expr() for most specific overload (type widening) 3) Fall back to first arity match. Uses existing func_params and TypeEnvironment.
title: Executor: arity + type-based overload resolution at call time

description: Integration tests: different arity overloads, same arity with type-based resolution, constructor overloading. Zero LLM calls.
title: Java: overload resolution integration tests

description: Integration tests: different arity overloads, same arity with type-based resolution. Zero LLM calls.
title: C#: overload resolution integration tests

description: Integration tests: different arity overloads, same arity with type-based resolution. Zero LLM calls. Default params are a separate concern.
title: Kotlin: overload resolution integration tests

description: Integration tests: different arity overloads, same arity with type-based resolution. Zero LLM calls.
title: Scala: overload resolution integration tests

description: Integration tests: different arity overloads, same arity with type-based resolution. Zero LLM calls.
title: C++: overload resolution integration tests

description: Integration tests: different arity overloads. Pascal uses explicit overload directive. Zero LLM calls.
title: Pascal: overload resolution integration tests

description: Optional: resolve overloads at lowering time when frontend has full visibility (intra-class calls). Emit CALL_METHOD targeting a specific label. Avoids runtime resolution for the common case. Depends on execution-time resolution being complete and tested.
title: Frontend lowering-time overload resolution (optional enhancement)

description: Deferred by user. Needs separate work to handle: C/C++ global array from void fn, Go tuple return, Lua multi-return unpacking, Pascal var params, Scala arr(i)->CALL_FUNCTION. Only 9/15 languages produce correct execution results.
title: Rosetta: multiple return values test

description: 129 remaining P1 frontend lowering gaps across 15 frontends, broken into 8 sub-epics by theme. From gap analysis (2026-03-10). 25 P0s ALL DONE, ~58 P1s DONE. See docs/frontend-lowering-gaps.md.
title: Frontend lowering: remaining P1 gaps (~147 across 15 frontends)

description: Pattern matching gaps across 5 languages (~32 gaps). Overlaps with red-dragon-jn0 (ADR-096). Python (7): class/complex/union/keyword/tuple/as patterns + pattern_list. C# (10): recursive/list/var/type/and/or/negated/relational/parenthesized/tuple patterns. Scala (4): capture/named_tuple/repeat/named patterns. Ruby (6): case_match, hash/array/find/match/test patterns, in_clause. Java (5): record/type patterns, guard, wildcard, underscore_pattern.
title: Cross-language pattern matching P1 gaps

TS-specific (7): instantiation_expression, ambient_declaration, function_signature, method_signature, property_signature, import_alias, import_require_clause. TS signatures partially addressed by ADR-100 Phase 1. Total: ~12 gaps.

description: function foo(x, ...args) should collect all remaining arguments into an array. Currently treats rest param as a single symbolic parameter. Requires VM-level support for variadic argument collection.

description: instantiation_expression, ambient_declaration, function_signature, method_signature, property_signature, import_alias, import_require_clause. method/property/call/construct/index signatures partially addressed by ADR-100 Phase 1c.
title: TypeScript-specific P1 gaps (7)

close_reason: Lower first non-type_arguments child as expression, discard type params (type erasure)
description: fn<string> — instantiation without calling. NOT a no-op: must lower the function child as an expression (LOAD_VAR etc.) and discard type_arguments (type erasure). Without a handler, falls through to SYMBOLIC. Pattern: extract function field, ctx.lower_expr() it, ignore type_arguments.
title: TS: instantiation_expression

close_reason: No-op: declare statements are purely type-level, emit nothing
description: declare module/function/class/var/enum/type/global — purely type-level, stripped by TS compiler. Verified no-op: emit nothing. All 9 child declaration variants produce zero runtime code.
title: TS: ambient_declaration

close_reason: No-op: overload signatures have no body/runtime code, emit nothing
description: Overload signatures at module level (no body, no runtime code). Verified no-op: emit nothing, like type_alias_declaration. The actual implementation comes from the function_declaration that follows.
title: TS: function_signature

close_reason: property_signature: CONST+STORE_VAR+seed. call/construct_signature: handled by _lower_ts_interface_method. index_signature: no-op (ADR-101)
description: Interface method and property signatures — partially addressed by ADR-100 Phase 1c
title: TS: method_signature and property_signature

description: Both have real runtime semantics. import Foo = Bar.Baz compiles to var Foo = Bar.Baz (LOAD_VAR/LOAD_FIELD chain + STORE_VAR). import x = require(y) compiles to var x = require(y) (CONST + CALL_FUNCTION + STORE_VAR). However, regular import_statement is already no-op in our frontend. Decision: treat consistently with other imports (no-op) or implement runtime bindings. Note: import_require_clause is child of import_statement; import_alias is standalone top-level declaration.
title: TS: import_alias and import_require_clause

description: C++17/C++20: coroutines (co_await, co_return, co_yield), templates (fold_expression, parameter_pack_expansion, template_instantiation), other (placeholder_type_specifier/auto, operator_name, destructor_name, structured_binding_declarator). Total: ~9 gaps.
title: C++ modern features P1 gaps

description: let x = ... && let y = ... chained conditions
title: Rust: let_chain

description: Skipped by user. Default parameter binding produces symbolic results for most languages during VM execution. Needs VM-level support for default param resolution.
title: Rosetta: default parameters test

description: 5 remaining ADR-096 gaps: Rust if-let/while-let/match (P1 #4-6), Scala match (P1 #9), C# switch patterns (P2 #13). All need conditional pattern matching with variable binding. Also ~25 pattern-matching P1s from lowering gap analysis across Python/C#/Scala/Ruby.
title: Pattern matching: 5 remaining ADR-096 gaps + ~25 P1s across frontends

description: In interpreter/frontends/php/declarations.py, lower_php_enum_case emits STORE_FIELD with constants.PARAM_SELF ('self') as the object operand instead of using a register from LOAD_VAR. This is the same bare-string pattern that was fixed for property declarations in the B2 field initializer work. The enum case should either LOAD_VAR the enum object or use the class-level register.
title: PHP lower_php_enum_case uses bare constants.PARAM_SELF in STORE_FIELD

description: Go frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION('slice', obj, low, high) for slice expressions. Currently goes symbolic. Need VM builtin for 2-arg slice (start, end) on strings, slices, and heap arrays.
title: VM builtin: slice expression for Go (a[low:high])

description: Rust uses range indexing (a[1..3], a[1..=3], a[..3]). Check if frontend emits slice calls and add VM support.
title: VM builtin: string/array slicing for Rust (a[1..3])

description: PHP lower_php_property_declaration() emits STORE_FIELD [self, $count, value] using constants.PARAM_SELF ('self'). But PHP methods receive the instance as '$this' (constants.PARAM_PHP_THIS). The mismatch means field initializers write to a non-existent 'self' variable, leaving heap fields unpopulated. Fix: use LOAD_VAR '$this' instead of 'self' in the STORE_FIELD emission. See _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py.
title: PHP field initializers use 'self' instead of '$this' in STORE_FIELD

description: When C++ declares an object on the stack (e.g. `Counter counter;`), no constructor is invoked. Unlike `new Counter()` which emits NEW_OBJECT + CALL_METHOD __init__, stack construction has no equivalent lowering. This means field initializers are never applied and method chaining on stack-constructed objects produces symbolic results. The frontend needs to detect variable declarations whose type is a known class and emit an implicit constructor call.
title: C++ stack-constructed objects don't trigger implicit constructor call

description: Scala array access (e.g. arr(0)) is lowered as CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX, because Scala's tree-sitter grammar represents array indexing as a function call. This causes the Rosetta array_accumulate test to skip for Scala. The frontend should detect the array-access pattern and emit LOAD_INDEX instead.
title: Scala array indexing emits CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX

description: Dropped: VM can't resolve len()/strlen()/.length/.len()/#s/Length() to concrete values for most languages (only Python, Go, Lua work). Needs built-in function resolution in VM executor.
title: Rosetta: string length test

description:
Track remaining languages where method chaining (obj.method().method()) still produces symbolic results instead of concrete values. Currently 11/14 class-capable languages work. Remaining 3 are blocked by independent issues:
- C++: stack construction doesn't trigger constructor (red-dragon-ng8)
- Lua: table-based OOP not tracked by VM (red-dragon-0vp)  
- Scala: implicit return from expression blocks (red-dragon-2j7)

This issue is resolved when all three blockers are fixed and the _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES skip list in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py is empty.
title: Method chaining fully concrete across all OOP-capable languages
